articles.title: U.S. Embassy in Baghdad again urges Americans to leave Iraq as Trump touts strikes on Iran
articles.description: In a post on Truth Social, President Trump claimed the U.S.  had "destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability." The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad, meanwhile, urged Americans to "leave Iraq immediately."

articles.title: WATCH:  Iranian-American journalists on the sentiment inside Iran as war rages
articles.description: Yeganeh Rezaian and Jason Rezaian discuss life in Iran and how they escaped an Iranian prison after being accused of being spies by the government. The Rezaians now reside in the U.S.

articles.title: FCC chair threatens broadcast licenses as Trump criticizes Iran war coverage

articles.title: Tehran threatens to retaliate after U.S. attacks vital Iranian oil hub

articles.description: Kharg Island is a small, heavily fortified, and strategically valuable island off Iran's northern coast.

articles.title: US embassy in Iraq hit by missile; no injuries reported
articles.description: The U.S. Embassy in Iraq was struck by a missile earlier on Saturday, with no reported injuries, several outlets reported. The missile attack destroyed the embassy’s air defence system, an Iraqi security source told Al Jazeera. The Associated Press also reported that the missile struck a helipad inside the compound. Agence France-Presse, however, reported that…

articles.title: Iranian lawmaker cites UN charter in calling Ukraine ‘legitimate target’ for helping Israel with drones
articles.description: The head of the Iranian parliament’s National Security Commission on Saturday threatened to attack Ukraine, claiming Kyiv had become a “legitimate target” by supplying drone technology to Israel. Ebrahim Azizi justified his warning by citing Article 51 of the United Nations charter, which recognizes a country’s right to individual or collective self-defense if an armed…

articles.title: Trump says ‘many countries’ will send war ships to patrol Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: President Trump on Saturday announced that “many countries” will send war ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing conflict with Iran, suggesting that its military is still capable of fighting back “no matter how badly defeated they are.” “Many Countries, especially those who are affected by Iran’s attempted closure of the Hormuz…

articles.title: The White House’s ‘memeification’ of war with Iran sparks scrutiny
articles.description: From “Grand Theft Auto” to “SpongeBob SquarePants,” the White House has turned to memes from pop culture and video games to sell President Trump’s war with Iran.  The videos follow a broader trend in the Trump administration’s social media strategy, which increasingly uses artificial intelligence, pop culture and at times sarcasm to promote Trump’s policy priorities…

articles.title: Iran’s exiled crown prince says he’s been in contact with Trump administration
articles.description: Iran’s exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi on Friday said he has been in contact with the Trump administration amid its conflict with Iran, which began with the killing of its former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Pahlavi, the son of Iran’s last shah who was ousted from power in 1979 during the Iranian Revolution, has…

articles.title: Trump says Iran is ‘totally defeated and wants a deal’
articles.description: President Trump late on Friday declared that Iran is “totally defeated” and “wants a deal” with the U.S., two weeks after the U.S. and Israel launched joint military operations on the Middle Eastern country. “The Fake News Media hates to report how well the United States Military has done against Iran, which is totally defeated…

articles.title: Iran threatens UAE cities after US attacked Kharg Island
articles.description: Iran’s military on Saturday threatened to attack multiple cities in the United Arab Emirates (UAE) that it claims the U.S. used in its attack on Kharg Island, a vital economic outpost. The claim made by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps’ (IRGC) Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters was that the U.S. launched its attack from “ports, docks…

articles.title: Ted Cruz, Tucker Carlson reignite feud over Iran war
articles.description: Cruz called Carlson a “demagogue” spreading antisemitism, days after Carlson called him and others who trust Israeli intelligence “dumbos.”

articles.description: Bill Maher challenges Gov. Josh Shapiro on Iran policy during "Real Time" debate about nuclear weapons and Middle East military involvement.

articles.title: Zelensky frustrated by stalled talks since Iran war, says US should put pressure on Putin, ‘not on me’
articles.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky expressed frustration that the Iran war has stalled his own country's peace talks -- and that President Trump wasn't putting more pressure on Russian tyrant Vladimir Putin to end the four-year bloodbath.

articles.title: Iranian foreign minister says there’s ‘no problem’ with new Supreme Leader
articles.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi insisted on Saturday that there is “no problem” with the country’s new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, despite his absence from the public eye. Khamenei, who was selected to succeed his father, Ali Khamenei, after the former Ayatollah was killed on the first day of the war, has not made any […]

articles.title: News coverage ‘distorting’ Operation Epic Fury success: Guy Benson
articles.description: Washington Examiner senior columnist Guy Benson said the news coverage of the Iran war does not reflect the success of the operations.  “We also heard from the president multiple points about how successful the mission has been in Iran, and based on a lot of the coverage you see, especially elsewhere, you would have no […]

articles.title: Coalition to send warships to Hormuz while US bombs ‘the hell out of the shoreline,’ Trump says
articles.description: President Donald Trump announced on Saturday that a multinational group is sending warships to defend the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian military forces blockading the oil shipping waterway. Trump’s announcement comes less than a day after he told reporters that the U.S. Navy could start to escort oil tankers through the narrow strait “very soon.” […]

articles.title: ‘Iran wants a deal’ but not good enough, Trump says
articles.description: President Donald Trump said that Iran wants to make a deal with the United States, but that it is not good enough for him to accept. “The Fake News Media hates to report how well the United States Military has done against Iran, which is totally defeated and wants a deal – But not a […]

articles.title: Bill Maher accuses Trump of being ‘caught off guard’ by Hormuz closure
articles.description: Political comedian and late-night talk show host Bill Maher blasted President Donald Trump’s handling of Iran’s continued attacks on the Strait of Hormuz, as the Trump administration says the Iranian military has been largely decimated. In particular, Maher accused the White House of being unprepared for an Iranian military blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. […]

articles.title: Iran faces financial death blow because of war
articles.description: Already strained by decades of sanctions and economic mismanagement, Iran faces a complete financial collapse as a result of its war with the U.S. and Israel. The U.S. and Israel’s attack against Iran has driven international trade, the energy industry, and much of the financial system to a halt, further worsening the economic situation of […]

articles.title: Government’s power to surveil foreign threats at risk over SAVE Act fight
articles.description: Fears of domestic Iranian terrorist cells may not be enough to get a clean extension for the federal government’s foreign spy powers that expire next month, as President Donald Trump and House conservatives complicate the path forward for congressional leaders. A push to attach the GOP’s voter ID and citizenship bill — the Safeguard American […]

analysis.summary: The US-Iran war, which began with joint US-Israeli strikes on February 28, escalated with US strikes on more than 90 military targets on Iran's Kharg Island oil hub, while Iran responded by closing the Strait of Hormuz to enemy vessels and threatening UAE cities. President Trump rejected ceasefire efforts while claiming Iran is 'totally defeated,' called for a multinational naval coalition to patrol the strait, and faced growing domestic and international criticism over the war's conduct and consequences.
agreements: The US struck more than 90 military targets on Iran's Kharg Island, a critical oil infrastructure hub
Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed to tankers and ships of enemies during the war
Trump called on multiple countries to send warships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz
Trump rejected ceasefire efforts while claiming Iran is 'totally defeated and wants a deal'
Iran's military threatened to attack UAE ports and cities it claimed the US used to launch Kharg Island attacks
Hamas urged Iran not to target neighboring countries while affirming Iran's right to self-defense
The US is sending additional Marines and warships from a Japan-based amphibious ready group to the Middle East
The war began with major joint US-Israel strikes announced on February 28
Former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on the first day of the war and succeeded by his son Mojtaba Khamenei
The US Embassy in Baghdad urged Americans to leave Iraq immediately due to kidnapping risks from Iran-aligned militias
The Iran war has stalled US-mediated Ukraine-Russia ceasefire talks
FCC Chair Brendan Carr threatened broadcast networks' licenses over critical Iran war coverage
Oil prices have risen significantly due to the conflict, pressuring US stock markets

disagreements.topic: Whether Iran is 'totally defeated' and its government at risk

positions.position: Frames Iran as facing a financial 'death blow' and presents Trump's military claims favorably, with commentary that news coverage distorts the success of operations
Reports US intelligence assessment that Iran's government is not at risk of collapse, contradicting Trump's 'totally defeated' rhetoric
Analysis shows Iranian drones are 'puncturing US and allied defenses,' presenting a new type of struggle, suggesting Iran retains significant military capability
Reports Bill Maher accused Trump of being 'caught off guard' by the Hormuz closure, suggesting the war is not going as well as claimed

disagreements.topic: Framing of civilian harm and humanitarian impact

positions.position: Highlights UNESCO condemning a US/Israeli strike on a girls' primary school in Minab, features Iranian civilian perspectives, and raises the question of Gaza being ignored amid the war
Publishes satellite analysis showing comprehensive structural damage across Iran, providing a broad picture of destruction
Focuses primarily on military targets and Iran's economic and military losses with minimal discussion of civilian impact
Focuses on military operations, air dominance, and strategic success with no reporting on civilian casualties

disagreements.topic: Framing of FCC threats against media

positions.position: Frames it as FCC chair threatening broadcast licenses in response to Trump criticizing Iran war coverage
Frames it as the Trump administration threatening news outlets over critical coverage, emphasizing press freedom concerns
Frames the issue as news coverage 'distorting' the success of Operation Epic Fury, implicitly justifying the administration's complaints

disagreements.topic: Whether the war aligns with US interests and strategic wisdom

positions.position: Argues Trump's vision of the Middle East is more in keeping with US interests than Obama's, while also noting Trump's 'muddled messaging'
Reports the war has exposed cracks in Trump's Republican coalition, with significant numbers of Republicans having serious doubts
Highlights the Cruz-Carlson feud as evidence of deep Republican divisions, with Carlson calling those who trust Israeli intelligence 'dumbos'
Reports critically on the White House's 'memeification' of war using pop culture and video games to promote military operations

disagreements.topic: Responsibility for the Strait of Hormuz crisis

positions.position: Emphasizes that geography and regional rivalries prevented Gulf countries from ever finding a true alternative to the strait, suggesting the vulnerability was long foreseeable
Frames it as Iranian aggression requiring a multinational coalition response, with Trump promising to 'bomb the hell out of the shoreline'
Frames Iran's closure as a wartime defensive measure, reporting Iran's position that the strait is closed to 'tankers and ships of enemies'

disagreements.topic: Impact of the missile strike on the US Embassy in Baghdad

positions.position: Reports the embassy was hit with no injuries, noting conflicting reports: Al Jazeera says the air defense system was destroyed, AP says a helipad was struck, and AFP reported the missile struck a different location
Focuses on the embassy urging Americans to leave Iraq, juxtaposed against Trump's Truth Social claim that the US had 'destroyed 100% of Iran's Military capability'

uniqueAngles.angle: Extensive coverage of civilian impact including UNESCO condemnation of a strike on a girls' school in Minab, international protests in Seoul, an Iranian woman's message to Americans, and editorial questioning whether the world is ignoring Gaza

Detailed analysis of 30 videos and satellite images showing Iranian drones successfully penetrating US and allied defenses, and economic reporting on how the war threatens to erase benefits from larger tax refunds
Oregon State University satellite analysis providing one of the most comprehensive maps of structural damage across Iran since the war began
Examination of why Gulf countries never built viable alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz despite decades of known vulnerability

Unique reporting on Iranian lawmaker citing UN charter to call Ukraine a 'legitimate target' for supplying drone technology to Israel, and critique of White House 'memeification' of the war using GTA and SpongeBob imagery
Reports Iran's exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi has been in contact with the Trump administration during the conflict
Focus on the Ted Cruz-Tucker Carlson feud over the war, with Cruz calling Carlson a 'demagogue' spreading antisemitism
Coverage of Senator Mark Kelly rebuking Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth for saying 'no quarter, no mercy,' language Kelly says violates laws of war

Reports on release of 172 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, US offering $10 million reward for information on Iran's new Supreme Leader, and framing China as having used Iran to distract the West
Reports on FBI alert warning of Iranian drone threats specifically in California and DOJ assessment that the war combined with FBI cuts raises domestic terror threats
Reports on the SAVE Act fight potentially jeopardizing government surveillance powers (FISA) needed to monitor foreign threats including Iranian terrorist cells
Highlights that US-mediated Russia-Ukraine ceasefire talks were paused specifically because of the Iran war, with a Russian strike killing 4 in Kyiv
Features Zelensky's frustration that the Iran war stalled Ukraine peace talks, quoting him saying Trump should put pressure on Putin 'not on me'
Reports on a Greek tanker hit by an unidentified object near a CPC terminal and India seeking passage for stranded vessels around the Strait of Hormuz
Analysis of how the Iran war exposed cracks in Trump's Republican coalition beyond just the Cruz-Carlson dispute
Features Iranian pop icon Googoosh saying Iran's freedom is within reach, profiles 'sea geeks' monitoring naval movements, and argues the Middle East is ready for Abraham Accords 2.0
Editorial arguing Arab nations' era of accommodating Iran is over and Washington should tell Gulf partners that neutrality is no longer a strategy
Detailed reporting on Iran's potential complete financial collapse due to war's impact on international trade, energy industry, and financial system
Coverage of Bill Maher pressing Democratic Gov. Josh Shapiro on whether he would 'still do nothing' about Iran's nuclear weapons program

analysis.summary: A man rammed his vehicle into Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan, in what the FBI is investigating as a targeted antisemitic act of violence against the Jewish community. The attacker, identified as a naturalized U.S. citizen from Lebanon who had recently lost family members in an Israeli strike on Lebanon, crashed into the building and started a fire, but dozens of children and staff inside survived thanks to security preparations and quick action by staff. The incident has sparked broader debates about domestic terrorism, antisemitism, and immigration policy.
agreements: A man rammed a vehicle into Temple Israel synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan
The attack is being investigated by the FBI as a targeted act of violence against the Jewish community
The attacker was a naturalized U.S. citizen originally from Lebanon
The attacker had recently lost family members in an Israeli military strike in Lebanon
Children and staff were inside the synagogue at the time but the attack was not deadly
Security preparations and quick response by staff helped prevent casualties
A fire was involved in addition to the vehicle ramming

disagreements.topic: Framing of the attacker's motivation

positions.position: Emphasizes the suspect's personal loss — family killed in an Israeli attack on Lebanon — as context for the attack
Frames the attack as 'Jihad in America' and part of a broader 'American Islamist threat,' arguing it goes beyond opposition to Israel
Links the attack to broader Middle East fighting and places it alongside other potentially related incidents like a Virginia university shooting
Frames it as part of a broader 'virus of antisemitic violence'

disagreements.topic: Whether the incident should prompt immigration policy discussion

positions.position: Explicitly argues against granting citizenship to people who 'hate America and wish to kill Americans,' connecting the attack to immigration policy
Does not frame the story as an immigration issue, focusing instead on security preparedness and the suspect's personal background
Notes the DHS statement that the attacker was a naturalized citizen from Lebanon but does not editorialize about immigration policy

disagreements.topic: Whether this is an isolated incident or part of a broader pattern of attacks

positions.position: Links the synagogue attack to a shooting at Old Dominion University in Virginia, suggesting both may be connected to Middle East fighting, and discusses 'sleeper cells' and lone attackers
Frames it in the context of increasing small-scale attacks on houses of worship more broadly
Frames it as part of a persistent American Islamist threat and also references a thwarted terror attack in New York

disagreements.topic: Characterization of the incident type

positions.position: Described it as an 'active shooter incident'
Described it primarily as a vehicle-ramming attack with fire, not a shooting

uniqueAngles.angle: Focuses specifically on how active shooter training conducted in January at the synagogue helped prepare staff to respond to the attack
Examines the attack's impact on the longstanding coexistence of Jewish, Arab, and Muslim communities in and around Detroit, testing community bonds built over more than a century
Connects the Michigan synagogue attack to a shooting at Old Dominion University in Virginia, framing both as potential consequences of Middle East conflict and raising concerns about 'sleeper cells'
Highlights the role of preschool teachers alongside security staff in protecting children during the attack
Details the suspect's personal loss of family members in an Israeli strike on Lebanon as a specific motivating factor
Publishes multiple pieces framing the attack within broader themes of Islamist extremism, immigration policy, and antisemitism within political movements
Credits 'years of planning and a bit of luck' for averting tragedy, focusing on the long-term security preparations of the temple

Dogu wrote on the…

articles.title: American flag raised at US Embassy in Venezuela for the 1st time since 2019
articles.description: The United States flag was seen raised over the U.S. Embassy in Venezuela for the first time since 2019.

analysis.summary: The U.S. Embassy in Venezuela raised the American flag on March 14, 2026, marking the first time in seven years since the flag was lowered on March 14, 2019. The reopening signals a restoration of diplomatic presence in Venezuela, though the context and motivations behind the move are framed differently by various outlets.

The flag had not flown at the embassy since March 14, 2019, making it approximately seven years
The event marks a significant diplomatic milestone in U.S.-Venezuela relations

Presents the story in straightforward, factual terms focused on the flag-raising itself, without attributing specific motivations

Does not characterize the nature of the Venezuelan government in the available content

stories.headline: Antisemitic attacks in Europe: Explosions at Jewish school in Amsterdam and Rotterdam synagogue

articles.title: Explosion at Jewish School in Amsterdam Is 2nd Antisemitic Attack in 2 Days, Officials Say
articles.description: No one was hurt in the explosion at a Jewish school in Amsterdam, or in an earlier one at a Rotterdam synagogue.

articles.title: Terror on the Upper East Side - The Free Press
articles.description: Terror on the Upper East Side  The Free Press

articles.title: The Virus of Antisemitic Violence - The Free Press
articles.description: The Virus of Antisemitic Violence  The Free Press

analysis.summary: Explosions targeted a Jewish school in Amsterdam and a synagogue in Rotterdam in what officials described as antisemitic attacks occurring within two days of each other. No one was reported injured in either incident. The attacks drew broader commentary about the spread of antisemitic violence.
agreements: Antisemitic violence is a significant and concerning issue

disagreements.topic: Framing and scope of the story

positions.position: Focuses specifically on the factual details of the two explosions in the Netherlands, reporting them as discrete news events with key details (no injuries, two-day span, official characterization as antisemitic).

Frames antisemitic violence in broader, more dramatic terms ('Terror,' 'Virus'), suggesting a wider pattern rather than reporting on isolated incidents. One article appears to reference a separate incident on New York's Upper East Side, linking multiple events together.

uniqueAngles.angle: Provides specific factual details: no injuries occurred, the Amsterdam target was a Jewish school, the Rotterdam target was a synagogue, and officials explicitly labeled the incidents as antisemitic attacks.

Appears to connect European antisemitic attacks to a broader global pattern, including an apparent incident on New York's Upper East Side, framing antisemitic violence as a spreading 'virus' rather than isolated events.

stories.headline: Oscars 2026: Predictions, new categories, and red carpet coverage

articles.title: WATCH:  Most iconic and unexpected moments in Oscar history
articles.description: ABC News’ Will Ganss looks back at some of the most memorable moments in the Academy Awards’ nearly 100-year history.

articles.title: WATCH:  1st-time Oscar nominee is a retired high school English teacher
articles.description: ABC News' Danny New talks to Robert Kaplow, writer of "Blue Moon," which has been nominated for best original screenplay.

articles.title: WATCH:  Sneak-peak at the performances at the Oscars
articles.description: "GMA's" Lara Spencer spoke with Mandy Moore, an Emmy award-winning choreographer, for a preview of the big performances at the Oscars.

articles.title: Oscars 2026 predictions
articles.description: Kat Rosenfield, a culture writer for the Free Press, joins "CBS Saturday Morning" to preview the 98th Academy Awards and discuss her new novel, "How to Survive in Woods." The Free Press is a Paramount publication.

articles.title: The Man Behind the Oscars ‘Glambot’ on the Red Carpet
articles.description: Cole Walliser grabs celebrities for red-carpet close-ups. In recent years, he has become known in his own right, for better or for worse.

articles.title: Prediction Markets? An 83% Chance That Oscars Pundits Hate Them.
articles.description: Online wagering is all the rage. But the crowdsourced data generally doesn’t interest experts who have built a brand predicting Academy Awards races.

articles.title: What to Watch for at the 2026 Oscars: Best Actor Race, Conan O’Brien and More
articles.description: It will be a tight race for best picture and actor, while the In Memoriam segment looks to be supersized this year.

articles.title: On the lot: Pre-Oscars buzz and another look at the PR wars in Hollywood
articles.description: In today’s edition: Academy Awards warm-ups with Conan O’Brien, the Rebel Wilson case with a Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni connection, a new Netflix docuseries from Liz Garbus that will make you glad you’re not a Murdoch, and this week’s recommendations from industry insiders.

articles.title: This year’s Oscars will include a brand-new category: Who’s up for the award?
articles.description: For the first time in more than two decades, the Academy Awards is introducing an all-new competitive category at the Oscars.

articles.title: Sentimental Value Avoids Contemporary Politics
articles.description: An Oscar-favored European drama excuses Hollywood’s sins.

analysis.summary: Coverage of the 2026 (98th) Academy Awards spans predictions for tight best picture and best actor races, the introduction of a new competitive category for casting (the first new category in over two decades), red carpet features, and previews of performances and host Conan O'Brien. Sources vary widely in their angles, from entertainment previews to cultural criticism.
agreements: The 2026 ceremony is the 98th Academy Awards
Conan O'Brien is hosting the Oscars
The best picture and best actor races are considered competitive this year

positions.position: Highlights tension between online prediction markets and traditional Oscar pundits, noting experts who have built brands predicting the Awards generally dismiss crowdsourced wagering data
Presents straightforward Oscar predictions from a culture writer without addressing prediction markets at all

disagreements.topic: Framing of Oscar-nominated films

positions.position: Frames 'Sentimental Value,' an Oscar-favored European drama, as a way for Hollywood to avoid contemporary politics and excuse its own sins
Focuses on the competitive horse race of best picture and best actor without ideological critique of the nominated films

disagreements.topic: Emphasis on celebrity and spectacle vs. substance

positions.position: Emphasizes performances, historic spectacle moments, and human-interest stories like a retired teacher earning a nomination
Balances celebrity coverage (Glambot) with analytical pieces on prediction methodology and key races
Situates the Oscars within broader Hollywood industry dynamics, including PR wars, the Rebel Wilson case, and the Blake Lively/Justin Baldoni connection

uniqueAngles.angle: Only source reporting on the introduction of a brand-new competitive Oscar category for casting, the first new category in more than two decades
Features Robert Kaplow, a retired high school English teacher and first-time Oscar nominee for best original screenplay for 'Blue Moon'
Previews Oscar performances with Emmy-winning choreographer Mandy Moore
Profiles Cole Walliser, the man behind the 'Glambot' slow-motion red carpet camera, and his rise to fame in his own right
Explores the clash between online prediction markets and established Oscar pundits who dismiss crowdsourced data
Notes the In Memoriam segment is expected to be supersized this year
Connects pre-Oscars coverage to broader Hollywood stories including the Rebel Wilson legal case, Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni, and a new Netflix Murdoch docuseries
Critiques 'Sentimental Value' from a culturally conservative perspective, arguing the European drama lets Hollywood avoid engaging with contemporary politics
Culture writer Kat Rosenfield discusses both Oscar predictions and her new novel 'How to Survive in Woods,' noting The Free Press is a Paramount publication

articles.title: Trump urges other nations to help secure shipping through Strait of Hormuz - Reuters

articles.title: Trump says ‘many countries’ will send war ships to patrol Strait of Hormuz

articles.title: Coalition to send warships to Hormuz while US bombs ‘the hell out of the shoreline,’ Trump says
articles.description: President Donald Trump announced on Saturday that a multinational group is sending warships to defend the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian military forces blockading the oil shipping waterway. Trump’s announcement comes less than a day after he told reporters that the U.S. Navy could start to escort oil tankers through the narrow strait “very soon.” […]

analysis.summary: President Trump announced that a multinational coalition of countries will send warships to the Strait of Hormuz in response to Iran's blockade or attempted closure of the critical shipping waterway. The announcement comes amid an ongoing military conflict between the US and Iran, with Trump suggesting the US Navy would escort oil tankers through the strait.
